User Story: As a user I create a workspace so my memories live in a tenant I own.
Expected Behaviour: create_workspace(name) inserts a workspaces row owned by ctx.sender plus a workspace_members row role=owner; visible to caller via my_workspaces.
Edge Cases: Empty name accepted (no length validation); duplicate names allowed; each call mints a new autoInc id.
Test Cases: Create workspace; assert my_workspaces returns it; assert caller is owner member.
Notes: qa_smoke STDB-001; ws id 4 created in run.

User Story: As a user I capture a thought and have it stored as an unverified candidate memory.
Expected Behaviour: capture_with_embedding(ws,content,source,kind): HTTP embed (outside tx) then tx writes capture_event + continuity_object(status=candidate,trust=unverified) + revision 1; returns embeddingRef.
Edge Cases: Non-member caller rejected (SenderError); empty content allowed; embed endpoint is a stub until a real provider is wired.
Test Cases: Capture into own ws; assert object is candidate/unverified and embedding_ref set.

User Story: As a user I promote a candidate into durable memory with an explicit trust class.
Expected Behaviour: commit_memory(objectId,trustClass): membership-checked; sets status=committed and trust_class to caller-supplied value.
Edge Cases: Unknown objectId -> error; non-member -> SenderError; trust class is free-text (no enum enforcement).

User Story: As a user I correct a memory and keep an auditable revision history.
Expected Behaviour: correct_memory(objectId,newContent): adds revision, supersedes prior (superseded_by), repoints current_revision_id, sets trust=user_confirmed, resets embedding_ref for re-embed.
Edge Cases: Unknown objectId -> error; first correction creates revision 2; embedding_ref cleared (stale vector).
Test Cases: Correct committed object; assert trust=user_confirmed and embedding_ref reset to ''.

User Story: As a user I track open threads and resolve them.
Expected Behaviour: open_loop_create(ws,label,posture,objectId) inserts status=open; open_loop_resolve(loopId) sets status=resolved + resolvedAt; both membership-checked.
Edge Cases: objectId=0 means no link; resolving unknown loop -> error; resolvedAt nullable until resolved.
Test Cases: Create then resolve a loop; assert status transitions open->resolved.

User Story: As a user I record entities and relationships my memories refer to.
Expected Behaviour: create_entity(ws,kind,name) and link_entities(ws,src,dst,relation) insert membership-checked rows; readable via my_entities / my_entity_edges.
Edge Cases: Self-links allowed; no dedupe of entities; relation is free-text.
Test Cases: Create 2 entities, link them, assert edge relation persisted.

User Story: As a user/system I attach trust signals to a memory.
Expected Behaviour: record_trust_signal(ws,objectId,type,weight,note) inserts membership-checked row; readable via my_trust_signals.
Edge Cases: weight is i32 (negative allowed); type free-text; no aggregation/rollup implemented.
Test Cases: Record a signal; assert it appears in my_trust_signals.

User Story: As a user I flag two memories as contradictory and resolve the case.
Expected Behaviour: open_contradiction(ws,a,b,note) inserts status=open; resolve_contradiction(caseId,resolution) sets status + resolvedAt; membership-checked.
Edge Cases: resolution is free-text (resolved_a/resolved_b/dismissed by convention, not enforced); unknown case -> error.
Test Cases: Open then resolve a contradiction; assert status=resolved_a.

User Story: As a user I search my memories by keyword and get ranked hits.
Expected Behaviour: recall_lexical(ws,query,limit): membership-checked; scores non-superseded objects by whole-word(2)/substring(1) overlap with current-revision content, ranks in TS, returns top-N JSON.
Edge Cases: Zero-score rows filtered (-> []); /sql has no ORDER BY so ranking is in TS; superseded/rejected excluded; limit truncates after ranking.
Test Cases: Query matching token -> hit; nonsense token -> []; limit=1 truncates to top-1 (009c); non-member recall rejected (015c).

User Story: As a workspace owner I store the embedding provider credential server-side.
Expected Behaviour: set_provider_key(ws,provider,apiKey,embedEndpoint,storeEndpoint,model): owner-only; upserts into the PRIVATE provider_keys table.
Edge Cases: Non-owner -> SenderError; replaces existing row for same provider; private table not readable by non-owner clients.
Test Cases: Owner sets key (200); non-owner set_provider_key rejected (010b); non-owner cannot read provider_keys.
Notes: qa_smoke STDB-010 + 010b (non-owner rejected); isolation re-verified via non-owner /sql denial.

User Story: As a user I index a memory into an external vector store via a procedure.
Expected Behaviour: embed_semantic(ws,objectId,provider): tx1 reads content+key, HTTP embed with key in Authorization (key sourced from private table, never an arg), tx2 stores embeddings row + sets embedding_ref.
Edge Cases: Key never a call arg; non-member -> SenderError; unknown object -> error; float-packing + external-store upsert are TODO (placeholder bytes today).
Test Cases: Embed an object; assert authEcho='Bearer <key>' and embedding_ref=ext:<provider>:<ws>:<obj>.
Notes: qa_smoke STDB-011; verified vs httpbin echo. TODO: real provider float packing + store upsert.

User Story: As a user I search memories semantically via an external vector store.
Expected Behaviour: recall_semantic(ws,query,provider,limit): embed query (HTTP), query external store for nearest ids (HTTP), tx reads those rows by id, returns ranked JSON.
Edge Cases: Without a real store returns [] (no neighbor ids); read-by-id mirrors recall_lexical; membership-checked.
Test Cases: Call with stub store -> []; (full path needs a real vector store).
Notes: Orchestration verified; neighbor-quality seam unverified pending real store. Not a defect — incomplete feature.

User Story: As a user/system I re-embed an existing object.
Expected Behaviour: embed_via_http(objectId): HTTP fetch then withTx updates embedding_ref after membership check.
Notes: qa_smoke STDB-013 (now executed in harness; e.g. vec:http:200:8).

User Story: As a workspace owner I grant another identity access.
Expected Behaviour: add_member(ws,member,role): owner-only; idempotent; inserts workspace_members row so the member passes assertMember.
Edge Cases: Non-owner -> SenderError; already-member -> no-op; identity arg requires typed SDK encoding.
Test Cases: Owner grants member; member then passes a membership-gated write.
Notes: Verified earlier via typed SDK (Track A loop); harness identity owns its ws so not re-run today.

User Story: As a user I only ever read/write rows in my own workspaces.
Expected Behaviour: Canonical tables private; public views filter by caller workspace membership; reducers/procedures membership-check before any write; non-owner raw SQL on private tables denied.
Edge Cases: Non-member sees empty views; non-member writes (capture) and reads (recall) rejected; owner SQL bypass exposes private tables (see STDB-016).
Test Cases: Fresh non-owner token: private SELECT denied + my_* empty (curl); non-member capture rejected (015b); non-member recall rejected (015c); my_workspace_members shows owner (015d); 2nd identity sees none of A's rows (015e).
Notes: qa_smoke 015b/015c/015d/015e + session curl. Reducer/procedure-level denial now executed, not just SQL-read.

User Story: As the database operator I can read private tables; clients cannot.
Expected Behaviour: The publishing/owner identity bypasses private-table visibility in /sql; non-owner clients are denied.
Edge Cases: provider_keys.api_key readable by owner via SQL — secret vs clients, NOT vs operator.
Test Cases: Owner SELECT provider_keys -> rows; non-owner -> denied.
Notes: Documented in ADR isolation section. Design property, not a leak to clients.

User Story: As a client I provision a per-user identity+token to authenticate.
Expected Behaviour: POST /v1/identity returns {identity,token}; Bearer token -> ctx.sender on /call and /sql.
Edge Cases: Anonymous identity has no workspace until it creates one; token must be treated as a secret.
Test Cases: Provision identity; use token to create+read a workspace.
Notes: qa_smoke provisions a fresh identity each run.

User Story: As an operator I evolve the schema without losing data.
Expected Behaviour: Adding tables/reducers/procedures = safe (data preserved). Dropping tables/columns = forbidden; only --delete-data overrides (full wipe). Incremental migration is additive-only.
Edge Cases: work_queue/artifacts cannot be dropped without wiping db; embedding_ref type cannot be changed in place.
Test Cases: Republish with additive change -> data preserved (observed across multiple publishes).
Notes: Cited in ADR Migrations section. Keep legacy tables until a clean rebuild.

User Story: As Alice's Python surfaces I drive the module over plain HTTP.
Expected Behaviour: alice_spacetime_reference.py: provision_identity, call (reducer), call_proc (procedure body), sql (views). Defaults to maincloud/alice-continuity.
Edge Cases: No retry/backoff; raises on non-200; token handling left to caller.
Test Cases: Run reference client end-to-end; assert capture+commit+recall succeed.

User Story: As an API client I capture/commit/correct/recall memories over the FastAPI backend.
Expected Behaviour: Implemented in apps/api (subset of 289 endpoints); Postgres + pgvector + RLS.
Test Cases: Phases 1-2 pending; Phase 3 not executable here (needs Postgres, excluded by no-local-DB).
Notes: Slated for re-point onto SpacetimeDB (Track B). Execution blocked by no-local-DB rule.

As an API client I manage tasks.
As an API client I manage approval workflows.
As a user I connect Gmail/Calendar.
As a user I configure sources, schedules, connectors.
